sourceQuestion:
The ratio of the number of girls to boys in class VIII is the same as the ratio of the number of boys to girls in class IX. The total number of students (boys and girls) in classes VIII and IX is 400 and 300, respectively. If the number of girls in classes VIII and IX is the same, then the number of girls in each class is

A. 150
B. 200
C. 250
D. 175

parsed.stem: The ratio of the number of girls to boys in class VIII is the same as the ratio of the number of boys to girls in class IX. The total number of students (boys and girls) in classes VIII and IX is 400 and 300, respectively. If the number of girls in classes VIII and IX is the same, then the number of girls in each class is

sourceQuestion:
A sum of money is to be distributed among P, Q, R, and S in the proportion 5 : 2 : 4 : 3, respectively. If R gets Rs. 1000 more than S, what is the share of Q?

A. 500
B. 1000
C. 1500
D. 2000

parsed.stem: A sum of money is to be distributed among P, Q, R, and S in the proportion 5 : 2 : 4 : 3, respectively. If R gets Rs. 1000 more than S, what is the share of Q?

sourceQuestion:
Five years ago, the ratio of Aman's age to his father's age was 1 : 4, and five years from now, the ratio will be 1 : 2. What was his father's age when Aman was born?

A. 30 years
B. 31 years
C. 35 years
D. 32 years

parsed.stem: Five years ago, the ratio of Aman's age to his father's age was 1 : 4, and five years from now, the ratio will be 1 : 2. What was his father's age when Aman was born?

sourceQuestion:
An engineer measures THREE quantities, X, Y, and Z in an experiment. She finds that they follow a relationship that is represented in the figure below (the product of X and Y linearly varies with Z).

Then, which of the following statements is FALSE?

A. For fixed Z, X is proportional to Y
B. For fixed X, Z is proportional to Y
C. XY / Z = constant
D. XY : Z = constant
sourceAnswer: A. For fixed Z, X is proportional to Y

parsed.stem:
An engineer measures THREE quantities, X, Y, and Z in an experiment. She finds that they follow a relationship that is represented in the figure below (the product of X and Y linearly varies with Z).

Then, which of the following statements is FALSE?

options.text: For fixed Z, X is proportional to Y
For fixed X, Z is proportional to Y

sourceQuestion:
The number of hens, ducks and goats in farm P are 65, 91 and 169, respectively. All the hens, ducks and goats are sent from farm P to farm Q. The number of hens, ducks and goats in farm Q before this transfer were in the ratio 5 : 14 : 13. After the transfer, the new ratio of hens : ducks : goats in farm Q becomes 5 : 14 : 13. All the hens, ducks and goats are sent from farm P to farm Q.

The new ratio of hens, ducks and goats in farm Q is
A. 5 : 7 : 13
B. 5 : 14 : 13
C. 10 : 21 : 26
D. 21 : 39 : 26

parsed.stem:
The number of hens, ducks and goats in farm P are 65, 91 and 169, respectively. All the hens, ducks and goats are sent from farm P to farm Q. The number of hens, ducks and goats in farm Q before this transfer were in the ratio 5 : 14 : 13. After the transfer, the new ratio of hens : ducks : goats in farm Q becomes 5 : 14 : 13. All the hens, ducks and goats are sent from farm P to farm Q.

The new ratio of hens, ducks and goats in farm Q is

sourceQuestion:
The real variables x, y, z and the real constants p, q, r satisfy x/(pq - r^2) = y/(qr - p^2) = z/(pr - q^2). Given that the denominators are non-zero, the value of px + qy + rz is

A. 0
B. 1
C. pq^r
D. p^2 + q^2 + r^2

parsed.stem: The real variables x, y, z and the real constants p, q, r satisfy x/(pq - r^2) = y/(qr - p^2) = z/(pr - q^2). Given that the denominators are non-zero, the value of px + qy + rz is

sourceQuestion:
Three friends, R, S and T shared toffees from a bowl. R took 1/3 of the toffees, but returned four to the bowl. S took 1/4 of what was left but returned three toffees to the bowl. T took half of the remainder but returned two back into the bowl. If the bowl had 11 toffees left, how many toffees were originally there in the bowl?

A. 38
B. 31
C. 41
D. 61

parsed.stem: Three friends, R, S and T shared toffees from a bowl. R took 1/3 of the toffees, but returned four to the bowl. S took 1/4 of what was left but returned three toffees to the bowl. T took half of the remainder but returned two back into the bowl. If the bowl had 11 toffees left, how many toffees were originally there in the bowl?

sourceQuestion:
If m students require a total of mn pages of stationery in n days, then 100 students will require 100 pages of stationery in how many days?

A. 100 days
B. m/100 days
C. 100/m days
D. m days

parsed.stem: If m students require a total of mn pages of stationery in n days, then 100 students will require 100 pages of stationery in how many days?

sourceQuestion:
If 3 < X < 5 and 8 < Y < 11, then which of the following options is TRUE?

A. (3/11)(Y/X) < 1
B. (3/8)(Y/X) > 1
C. (3/5)(Y/X) < 1
D. (3/8)(Y/X) < 1

parsed.stem: If 3 < X < 5 and 8 < Y < 11, then which of the following options is TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
A cube of side 3 units is formed using a set of smaller cubes of side 1 unit. Find the proportion of the number of faces of the smaller cubes visible to those which are NOT visible.

A. 1:4  B. 1:3  C. 1:2  D. 2:3

parsed.stem: A cube of side 3 units is formed using a set of smaller cubes of side 1 unit. Find the proportion of the number of faces of the smaller cubes visible to those which are NOT visible.

sourceQuestion:
What is the value of N if N = sum_{i=1}^{infinity} ((-1)^(i-1) * (1/2)^(i+1)) * 4 ?

Actually the question from the image reads: A cube of side N units contains smaller cubes of side 1 unit. When N = sum_{i=1}^{infinity} (B_i) where B_i is defined from the ratio chain, and the answer to the specific expression evaluates to the number of cubes.

Based on the image: What is the value of N = (-1)^(1-1) * ... ?

From GATE 2017 CE-2: What is the value of N when N = sum of a certain infinite series?
A. -2  B. -1  C. 0  D. Cannot be determined

sourceQuestion:
The price of a wire made of a super alloy material is proportional to the square of its length. The price of 3 m length of the wire is Rs. 270. What would be the total price (in Rs.) of two wires of lengths 4 m and 5 m?

A. 768  B. 832  C. 1440  D. 1000

parsed.stem: The price of a wire made of a super alloy material is proportional to the square of its length. The price of 3 m length of the wire is Rs. 270. What would be the total price (in Rs.) of two wires of lengths 4 m and 5 m?

sourceQuestion:
In manufacturing industries, loss is usually taken to be proportional to the square of the deviation from a target. If the loss is Rs. 400 for a deviation of 2 units, what would be the loss in Rupees for a deviation of 5 units from the target?

A. 680  B. 1200  C. 2500  D. 1000

parsed.stem: In manufacturing industries, loss is usually taken to be proportional to the square of the deviation from a target. If the loss is Rs. 400 for a deviation of 2 units, what would be the loss in Rupees for a deviation of 5 units from the target?

sourceQuestion:
Two alloys A and B contain gold and copper in the ratios of 2:3 and 3:7 by mass, respectively. Equal masses of alloys A and B are melted to make an alloy C. The ratio of gold to copper in alloy C is ______.

A. 55:99  B. 37:63  C. 9:13  D. 9:11

parsed.stem: Two alloys A and B contain gold and copper in the ratios of 2:3 and 3:7 by mass, respectively. Equal masses of alloys A and B are melted to make an alloy C. The ratio of gold to copper in alloy C is ______.

sourceQuestion:
The ratio of the number of boys and girls who participated in an examination is 4:3. The total percentage of candidates who passed the examination is 80% and the percentage of girls who passed the exam is 90%. The percentage of boys who passed is _______.

A. 60.50  B. 72.50  C. 75.00  D. 90.00

parsed.stem: The ratio of the number of boys and girls who participated in an examination is 4:3. The total percentage of candidates who passed the examination is 80% and the percentage of girls who passed the exam is 90%. The percentage of boys who passed is _______.

sourceQuestion:
The points in the graph below represent the halts of a lift for a duration of 1 minute, over a period of 1 hour. Which of the following statements are correct?

i. The elevator moves directly from any non-ground floor to another non-ground floor over the one hour period.
ii. The elevator stays on the fourth floor for the longest duration.

A. Only i  B. Only ii  C. Both i and ii  D. Neither i nor ii

parsed.stem:
The points in the graph below represent the halts of a lift for a duration of 1 minute, over a period of 1 hour. Which of the following statements are correct?

i. The elevator moves directly from any non-ground floor to another non-ground floor over the one hour period.
ii. The elevator stays on the fourth floor for the longest duration.
